$comment: Self-describing manifest for the Finish It capability-pipeline PLUGIN — a packaged, portable form of the pipeline mapped by the repo's pipeline.json. Copilot CLI loads the bundled skills from agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (the format proven in this repo); this file is the human/agent-readable package descriptor (analogous to pipeline.json) and the contract the install-pipeline skill follows. It does NOT duplicate skill logic — that lives in skills/*.md. See README.md.
name: finish-it-pipeline
description: Autonomous QA->fix capability pipeline as an installable plugin: bundles the local agent skills and an install-pipeline skill that scaffolds the GitHub-Actions CI half (which cannot be plugin runtime code) into a target repo.

ciTemplates.$comment: Installed into the target repo by skills/install-pipeline.md. Inert inside the plugin: GitHub Actions only runs workflows under the repo-root .github/workflows/. These are stages 3-6 (auto-fix, quality-gate, fix-loop, review) + the Copilot env setup — the part a harness plugin can ship + install but cannot BE.

requiredSecrets.COPILOT_AGENT_PAT: Fine-grained user PAT (metadata: read; actions, contents, issues, pull requests: read & write) owned by the Copilot-seat holder. The default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot assign Copilot or @copilot-mention it. Human-set; a plugin cannot carry it.
requiredSecrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: Optional — gate/research pings. Remove the notify job from quality-gate.yml if unused.

params.$comment: Repo-specific knobs the installer rewrites if the target app is not a single index.html with tests under tests/.

notes: Stages 3-6 stay on GitHub (event-triggered Actions, executed by the Copilot cloud agent + Node/Playwright). A harness plugin can ship and install them, but cannot BE them. See README.md -> 'What can and can't be a plugin'.
